result.summary: Alpaca exposes HTTP APIs for Trading and Broker integrations, a public MCP endpoint at https://mcp.alpaca.markets/mcp, and an `alpaca` CLI for Trading/Market Data workflows.
result.description: Alpaca provides brokerage infrastructure and APIs for trading stocks, options, crypto, market data, and embedded brokerage products. Its platform serves both direct traders using the Trading API and businesses building brokerage applications with the Broker API.

trading_oauth.label: Alpaca Trading account OAuth login
trading_oauth.setup: Create a Trading API account from [Trading API signup](https://app.alpaca.markets/signup). For CLI access, install the CLI and run `alpaca profile login`; the docs/blog state this signs you in with OAuth for paper trading and market data. For direct app integrations, start from [Alpaca OAuth overview](https://alpaca.markets/oauth) and the [Authentication docs](https://docs.alpaca.markets/us/docs/authentication).

trading_api_key.label: Alpaca Trading API key pair
trading_api_key.setup: Create a Trading API account from [Trading API signup](https://app.alpaca.markets/signup), then generate paper or live API credentials from the Trading Dashboard as described in [How to Get Alpaca's Trading API Key and Start Connecting](https://alpaca.markets/learn/connect-to-alpaca-api) and the [Authentication docs](https://docs.alpaca.markets/us/docs/authentication). Alpaca refers to these as API keys and secret keys; use the pair your account issues.

broker_api_key.label: Alpaca Broker API credentials
broker_api_key.setup: Create a Broker account from [Broker signup](https://broker-app.alpaca.markets/sign-up), then use the Broker Dashboard credentials flow referenced in [Credentials Management](https://docs.alpaca.markets/us/docs/credential-management) and [Getting Started with Broker API](https://docs.alpaca.markets/us/docs/getting-started-with-broker-api) to create your Broker API credentials.
